frontmatter_compliance.notes: All required fields present; valid semver; description 958 chars folded (under 1024); > is YAML scalar marker (permitted); allowed-tools hyphenated; owns empty for refs-only skill; composes_with lists real local skills; requires_claude_code true (correct — needs Bash).

description_quality.notes: Action verb (Clean up everything stale), enumerates capabilities, 16+ trigger variants, proactive trigger when git worktree list shows extras, mentions flags. 958 chars at upper end but within ceiling.

progressive_disclosure.notes: Body 199 lines / ~1400 words. Two references linked with explicit when-to-read guidance. Both refs under 300-line TOC threshold.

instruction_clarity.notes: Numbered Steps 1-7 with imperative voice. Each step explains WHY. 'Classification IS the safety net' framing excellent. Empty-category rule thoughtful UX.

coordination.notes: composes_with [git-commit, git-pr] both exist and accurate. spawned_by empty correct. owns correctly empty.

completeness.notes: All workflow content self-contained with concrete git commands. Edge Cases section covers nothing-to-clean, permission errors, squash-merge, large counts. Safety rules explicit. Both reference files exist and address gaps.

anti_patterns.notes: Safety rules enumerate 5 destructive-action constraints with rationale. Default behavior is scan→plan→confirm. --force use explained. Never assumes 'main'. No emojis, no hardcoded paths.

nits.item: Description 958 chars uses 16+ trigger variants; could trim to ~700 chars without losing coverage
nits.fix: Drop 'tidy git state', 'lots of things merged', 'I have too many branches', 'clean up my git' (covered by stronger variants).

nits.item: Description uses > folded scalar with semantically-meaningless line breaks
nits.fix: Consistency nit with other git/ skills.

nits.item: L17 references 'git-pr merges' but git-pr doesn't merge — slight misframing
nits.fix: Change 'after a run of git-pr merges several PRs in a row' to 'after a batch of PRs land'.

nits.item: L195-197 edge case 'Squash-merge not detected' references worktree rules but check also applies to branches
nits.fix: Make explicit that empty-diff classifier covers both branches without worktrees and worktrees themselves.

nits.item: L153-154 branch -d positional example could note xargs/for-loop for large batches
nits.fix: Add a note for batch deletion readability.

top_fixes.fix: Trim description from 958 → ~700 chars by removing redundant trigger variants
Clarify the 'git-pr merges' framing at L17 (git-pr doesn't merge)
Make explicit that squash-merge empty-diff classifier applies to branches too
